Name: Phase 0: Mobilize carve-out governance and TSA baseline
Notes: Programme launch, TSA framing, approved extension control, partner mobilisation, and QG1 concept approval under change request version 1.1.

Name: 0.1 Governance and partner mobilization
Notes: PMO setup with JCI, Bosch, KPMG, and Infosys.

Name: QG0 - Programme kickoff approved
Resource Names: KPMG PMO Lead + JCI Sponsor + Bosch Sponsor
Notes: Formal launch of the carve-out starting 2026-07-01. Users continue to work in the legacy JCI environment while Infosys builds the merger zone and the approved TSA extension remains available through 2027-07-31.

Name: Stand up PMO, governance cadence, and workstream RACI
Resource Names: KPMG PMO Lead + KPMG Project Manager
Notes: Weekly governance, steering, RAID, and reporting in place.

Name: Onboard Infosys for merger zone delivery and managed services
Resource Names: KPMG PMO Lead + Infosys Programme Manager
Notes: Delivery scope confirms merger zone setup, operations, and migration execution.

Name: Baseline TSA towers, service owners, and exit principles
Resource Names: KPMG Project Manager + JCI IT Manager + Bosch IT Manager
Notes: JCI TSA catalogue, approved extension through 2027-07-31, and exit logic defined for all in-scope IT towers.

Name: 0.2 Separation compliance and stakeholder alignment
Notes: Legal, privacy, communications, and regional alignment.

Name: Define legal perimeter, contracts, and supplier dependencies
Resource Names: KPMG Project Manager + JCI Legal Counsel + Bosch Legal Counsel
Notes: Commercial and legal separation perimeter agreed.

Name: Assess privacy, works council, and cross-border data constraints
Resource Names: KPMG Data Architect + JCI Legal Counsel
Notes: Regional data transfer and employee impact constraints captured.

Name: Launch site change network and stakeholder communications
Resource Names: KPMG Change Lead + JCI HR Lead + Bosch HR Lead
Notes: Regional comms and escalation channels activated across 48 sites.

Name: QG1 - Concept and transition model approved
Resource Names: KPMG PMO Lead + JCI Sponsor + Bosch Sponsor
Notes: Concept sign-off for JCI to merger zone to Bosch integration model.

Name: Phase 1: Discover landscape and design the merger zone
Notes: Detailed discovery of applications, SAP, sites, data, and target merger zone design.

Name: 1.1 Application, SAP, and data discovery
Notes: Discovery across more than 1,800 applications including major SAP scope.

Name: Catalogue 1,800-plus applications and assign transition disposition
Resource Names: KPMG Enterprise Architect + JCI Application Owner + Infosys Application Lead
Notes: Classify applications by retain, migrate, replace, retire, and TSA dependency.

Name: Assess SAP landscape, interfaces, and shared services exposure
Resource Names: KPMG SAP Architect + JCI SAP Owner + Infosys SAP Lead
Notes: Identify carve-out complexity across SAP systems, interfaces, and shared master data.

Name: Map dependencies and define migration waves for apps and data
Resource Names: KPMG Enterprise Architect + Infosys Application Lead
Notes: Wave sequencing aligns critical apps, regional apps, and local edge cases.

Name: 1.2 Merger zone and target-state architecture
Notes: Merger zone blueprint, security, connectivity, workplace, and Bosch end-state landing.

Name: Design merger zone hosting, network zones, and security blueprint
Resource Names: Infosys Infrastructure Architect + KPMG Infrastructure Architect
Notes: Target blueprint for temporary merger zone with secure JCI and Bosch connectivity.

Name: Design identity, Active Directory trust, and privileged access model
Resource Names: Infosys IAM Lead + KPMG Security Architect
Notes: Trust path and identity controls support JCI to merger zone to Bosch transition.

Name: Design workplace, M365, collaboration, and service management stack
Resource Names: Infosys Cloud Lead + KPMG Workplace Lead
Notes: Email, M365, Teams, endpoint management, and service desk operating design complete.

Name: Design Bosch landing zone integration for post-merger-zone operations
Resource Names: Bosch IT Manager + KPMG Infrastructure Architect + Infosys Programme Manager
Notes: Bosch target interfaces, identity landing, and support integration agreed.

Name: 1.3 TSA, migration, and site sequencing design
Notes: TSA service tower design, migration controls, and regional sequencing.

Name: Define TSA service towers, KPIs, and operational reporting
Resource Names: KPMG Project Manager + JCI IT Manager + Bosch IT Manager
Notes: Service lines and TSA metrics defined for transition governance with the approved seller-service buffer through 2027-07-31.

Name: Define exit criteria, acceptance tests, and service cutover checkpoints
Resource Names: KPMG Project Manager + Bosch IT Manager
Notes: Each tower receives explicit readiness and exit conditions.

Name: Define data separation controls and reconciliation approach
Resource Names: KPMG Data Architect + Infosys Data Migration Lead
Notes: Controls for business data separation, validation, and audit trail agreed.

Name: Sequence 48 sites and 12,000 users into migration waves
Resource Names: KPMG Deployment Lead + Infosys Service Delivery Lead
Notes: Regional wave plan balances business criticality and support capacity.

Name: Design command center, cutover, and hypercare support model
Resource Names: KPMG PMO Lead + Infosys Service Delivery Lead
Notes: Pre-GoLive command center and post-GoLive stabilization model defined.

Name: Phase 2: Build merger zone and prepare transition factory
Notes: Build core platforms, prepare migration factory, and complete test readiness for QG2 and QG3.

Name: 2.1 Merger zone platform build
Notes: Infrastructure, security, identity, workplace, and monitoring build.

Name: Provision merger zone hosting, compute, storage, and base services
Resource Names: Infosys Infrastructure Architect + KPMG Infrastructure Architect
Notes: Core hosting platform established for temporary operational landing zone.

Name: Deploy network connectivity, segmentation, and core security controls
Resource Names: Infosys Network Lead + Infosys Security Lead
Notes: Connectivity enables progressive site onboarding with required security segregation.

Name: Build identity federation, AD services, and privileged access tooling
Resource Names: Infosys IAM Lead + KPMG Security Architect
Notes: Core identity services ready for user migration into the merger zone.

Name: Stand up M365 tenant, collaboration tools, and service desk platform
Resource Names: Infosys Cloud Lead + Infosys Service Delivery Lead
Notes: Workplace and support stack prepared for all migrated users.

Name: Implement monitoring, backup, disaster recovery, and observability
Resource Names: Infosys Infrastructure Architect + KPMG Infrastructure Architect
Notes: Operational readiness controls active before broad migration starts.

Name: 2.2 SAP and application transition factory
Notes: SAP carve-out build plus app migration factory and tooling.

Name: Prepare SAP environments, copies, and carve-out configuration path
Resource Names: Infosys SAP Lead + KPMG SAP Architect
Notes: SAP technical path into merger zone prepared and validated.

Name: Execute SAP separation build, role redesign, and interface rewiring
Resource Names: Infosys SAP Lead + KPMG SAP Architect + JCI SAP Owner
Notes: Shared services, roles, and interfaces aligned to carved-out business scope.

Name: Build migration factory, tooling, and automation for application moves
Resource Names: Infosys Application Lead + Infosys Data Migration Lead
Notes: Migration tooling covers data movement, validation, and evidence collection.

Name: Build and validate wave 1 critical applications in merger zone
Resource Names: Infosys Application Lead + KPMG Enterprise Architect
Notes: Critical application stack prepared first for integrated testing.

Name: Pre-build wave 2 standard applications and package wave 3 edge apps
Resource Names: Infosys Application Lead + KPMG Enterprise Architect
Notes: Standard and specialist applications prepared for accelerated migration execution.

Name: 2.3 Site and user migration readiness
Notes: Endpoint readiness, local playbooks, and pilot readiness across 48 sites.

Name: Build device images, enrollment policies, and endpoint packaging
Resource Names: Infosys Workplace Lead + KPMG Workplace Lead
Notes: Endpoint build supports phased user migration into merger zone services.

Name: Prepare regional migration playbooks, floorwalker model, and logistics
Resource Names: KPMG Deployment Lead + Infosys Service Delivery Lead
Notes: Regional cutover packs and local support rosters ready.

Name: Run pilot site connectivity and service desk rehearsals
Resource Names: Infosys Service Delivery Lead + JCI IT Manager
Notes: Pilot proves merger zone onboarding process and support flows before scaled migration.

Name: 2.4 Test planning and go-no-go preparation
Notes: Formal test planning, mock rehearsals, and readiness evidence model.

Name: Build SIT and UAT plans, evidence templates, and sign-off workflow
Resource Names: KPMG Test Lead + Infosys QA Lead
Notes: Common evidence and defect lifecycle agreed for all workstreams.

Name: Run SAP mock cutover rehearsal 1 and capture rollback evidence
Resource Names: Infosys SAP Lead + KPMG SAP Architect
Notes: Initial mock validates timing, dependencies, and rollback controls.

Name: Complete operational readiness dossier for command center launch
Resource Names: KPMG PMO Lead + Infosys Programme Manager
Notes: Evidence package prepared for test-ready milestone and steering review.

Name: QG2 and QG3 - Build complete and test entry approved
Resource Names: KPMG PMO Lead + JCI Sponsor + Bosch Sponsor
Notes: Merger zone build complete and programme cleared for integrated testing and migration execution.

Name: Phase 3: Test, migrate to merger zone, and prepare Bosch landing
Notes: Formal testing, migration waves, Bosch operational readiness, and QG4 entry.

Name: 3.1 Formal testing and defect closure
Notes: Integrated testing, UAT, security, performance, DR, and regression closure.

Name: Execute end-to-end SIT across SAP, applications, and core services
Resource Names: Infosys QA Lead + KPMG Test Lead + Infosys SAP Lead
Notes: Full end-to-end process flows validated inside the merger zone.

Name: Run performance, security, and disaster recovery validation
Resource Names: Infosys Security Lead + Infosys Infrastructure Architect
Notes: Capacity, resilience, and control effectiveness validated for GoLive load.

Name: Run UAT with business teams across all regions and site groups
Resource Names: KPMG Test Lead + JCI Business Lead + Bosch Business Lead
Notes: Business users validate SAP and non-SAP journeys needed at cutover.

Name: Close defects, run regression, and obtain UAT closure evidence
Resource Names: Infosys QA Lead + KPMG Test Lead
Notes: Critical and high defects resolved before final readiness stage.

Name: 3.2 Migration waves into merger zone
Notes: Users, applications, and data move from JCI to the merger zone after the approved TSA buffer period while overall programme progress and GoLive remain unchanged.

Name: Migrate user waves 1 and 2 with workplace and identity cutover
Resource Names: Infosys Service Delivery Lead + Infosys Workplace Lead + JCI IT Manager
Notes: First half of the user base transitions from JCI to merger zone services.

Name: Migrate user waves 3 and 4 and complete 48-site onboarding
Resource Names: Infosys Service Delivery Lead + Infosys Workplace Lead + JCI IT Manager
Notes: All 12,000 users operate from the merger zone before QG4.

Name: Execute wave 1 application and data migration into merger zone
Resource Names: Infosys Application Lead + Infosys Data Migration Lead
Notes: Critical application and data moves completed with reconciliation.

Name: Execute wave 2 application and data migration into merger zone
Resource Names: Infosys Application Lead + Infosys Data Migration Lead
Notes: Standard application estate transitioned and validated.

Name: Execute wave 3 specialist application completion and data closure
Resource Names: Infosys Application Lead + Infosys Data Migration Lead
Notes: Specialist and regional edge applications completed before QG4.

Name: 3.3 Bosch landing and operating model readiness
Notes: Bosch acceptance of connectivity, support processes, and eventual integration path.

Name: Enable Bosch connectivity, identity trust, and operational acceptance
Resource Names: Bosch IT Manager + Infosys IAM Lead + Infosys Network Lead
Notes: Bosch confirms connectivity and identity patterns for downstream integration.

Name: Align resolver groups, service desk, and runbooks with Bosch operations
Resource Names: Infosys Service Delivery Lead + Bosch IT Manager
Notes: Support ownership model agreed for post-GoLive stabilization and later handover.

Name: Run final SAP cutover rehearsal and certify rollback readiness
Resource Names: Infosys SAP Lead + KPMG SAP Architect
Notes: Final rehearsal confirms cutover steps and fallback timing.

Name: 3.4 Pre-GoLive evidence pack
Notes: Final evidence pack proving all migration and readiness activities are complete.

Name: Reconcile all migrations and archive formal UAT sign-off
Resource Names: KPMG PMO Lead + Infosys Programme Manager
Notes: Users, applications, and data all reconciled before gate approval.

Name: Finalize infrastructure, SAP, and operational readiness dossier
Resource Names: Infosys Programme Manager + KPMG PMO Lead
Notes: Operations, security, SAP, and service evidence complete for steering.

Name: Prepare steering gate deck and cutover recommendation
Resource Names: KPMG PMO Lead + Bosch Sponsor + JCI Sponsor
Notes: Formal gate recommendation prepared for QG4 decision.

Name: QG4 - Pre-GoLive gate approved
Resource Names: KPMG PMO Lead + JCI Sponsor + Bosch Sponsor
Notes: Pre-GoLive approval granted; final readiness and open-item closure begins.

Name: Phase 4: Final readiness and GoLive decision
Notes: Mandatory buffer between QG4 and GoLive for closure, checks, rollback rehearsal, and sign-off.

Name: 4.1 Final readiness and open item closure
Notes: No migrations remain; only closure, checks, sign-offs, and command center activation.

Name: Resolve residual defects, confirm health checks, and secure business sign-off
Resource Names: KPMG Test Lead + Infosys Service Delivery Lead + Bosch Business Lead
Notes: All QG4 actions closed and no critical alerts remain before cutover.

Name: Activate cutover command center and issue final go decision
Resource Names: KPMG PMO Lead + Infosys Programme Manager + JCI Sponsor + Bosch Sponsor
Notes: Rollback path revalidated and final go decision issued before GoLive day 1.

Name: GoLive - Day 1 cutover to merger zone complete
Resource Names: KPMG PMO Lead + Infosys Programme Manager + JCI Sponsor + Bosch Sponsor
Notes: All in-scope users and services operate from the merger zone on GoLive.

Name: Phase 5: Hypercare, TSA exit, and Bosch handover
Notes: Ninety-day stabilization only; no new development or migration after GoLive.

Name: 5.1 Hypercare stabilization only
Notes: Support, hotfixes, enablement, and operational stabilization only.

Name: Run 24x7 incident triage and command center support
Resource Names: Infosys Service Delivery Lead + KPMG Project Manager
Notes: Round-the-clock stabilization support across all regions.

Name: Operate hotfix-only release cadence and eliminate recurring problems
Resource Names: Infosys Application Lead + Infosys SAP Lead
Notes: Bug fixes only after GoLive; no new features or migrations.

Name: Provide user enablement, local floorwalking, and adoption support
Resource Names: KPMG Change Lead + Infosys Workplace Lead
Notes: Support users in the new merger zone operating model after cutover.

Name: 5.2 TSA exit and Bosch operations handover
Notes: Tower-by-tower TSA exit followed by Bosch operational ownership uplift.

Name: Execute TSA exits by service tower and confirm acceptance
Resource Names: KPMG Project Manager + JCI IT Manager + Bosch IT Manager
Notes: JCI TSA services closed according to approved exit criteria and acceptance tests.

Name: Transfer knowledge, runbooks, and service ownership to Bosch operations
Resource Names: Bosch IT Manager + Infosys Programme Manager + KPMG Infrastructure Architect
Notes: Bosch operations absorb merger zone support knowledge and control model.

Name: Transition merger zone operating model to Bosch steady-state teams
Resource Names: Bosch IT Manager + Infosys Programme Manager
Notes: Operational ownership transitions from project mode to Bosch steady-state teams.

Name: 5.3 Governance closure and archive
Notes: Closure reporting, lessons learned, and artefact archive.

Name: Close financials, capture lessons learned, and archive artefacts
Resource Names: KPMG PMO Lead + KPMG Project Manager
Notes: Programme closure outputs completed and repository artefacts archived.

Name: QG5 - Project completion approved
Resource Names: KPMG PMO Lead + JCI Sponsor + Bosch Sponsor
Notes: Ninety-day hypercare complete, TSA exited, and handover to Bosch operations complete.
